content: The behaviour of real gas approaches ideal behaviour at
explanation: Real gases deviate from ideal gas behavior due to interactions between gas particles and the volume occupied by the particles themselves. As the pressure on a gas is reduced, the particles are farther apart, reducing interactions. Increasing the temperature provides more kinetic energy to the particles, overcoming intermolecular forces. Together, low pressure and high temperature conditions make the behavior of real gases more similar to that of an ideal gas, which is described by the ideal gas law without taking these interactions into account.

content: The ratio of average velocity of a gas molecule to the root mean square velocity at a particular temperature is
explanation:
The expression for root mean square velocity is

$$\left(\mu_{\mathrm{rms}}\right)=\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M}},$$

and for average speed, $$\left(\mu_{\mathrm{avg}}\right)=\sqrt{\frac{8 R T}{\pi M}}$$

For a particular gas, at particular temperature $$M$$ and $$T$$ are constant.

$$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad \frac{\mu_{\mathrm{avg}}}{\mu_{\mathrm{rms}}} & =\sqrt{\frac{8 R T}{\pi M}}: \sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M}} \\ & =\sqrt{2.54}: \sqrt{3} \\ & =1: 1.81 \end{aligned}$$

content: What is the molecular mass of X if the rate of diffusion of methane is twice that of X.
explanation:
Let, the rate of diffusion of gas $$X, r_1=b$$. Therefore, rate of diffusion of methane, $$r_2=2 b$$

According to Graham's law of diffusion,

$$\left(\frac{r_1}{r_2}\right)=\left(\sqrt{\frac{M_2}{M_1}}\right)$$

$$M_1=$$ molecular mass of gas $$X$$

$$M_2=$$ molecular mass of methane $$=16 \mathrm{~g}$$

$$\begin{array}{llrl} & \therefore & \frac{b}{2 b}=\sqrt{\frac{16}{M_2}} \\ \Rightarrow & \left(\frac{1}{4}\right)=\frac{16}{M_2} \\ \Rightarrow & M_2=64 \mathrm{~g} \end{array}$$

Therefore, molecular mass of $$X$$ is $$64 \mathrm{~g}$$.

content: What will be the density of N$$_2$$ gas at 230$$^\circ$$C and 3 atm pressure? (R = 0.082 L atm K$$^{-1}$$ mol$$^{-1}$$)
explanation:
We know, $$p V=n R T$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { or } p V=\frac{W}{M} R T\left[\text { Here, } n=\frac{\text { Weight of gas taken }(W)}{\text { Molar mass of gas }(M)}\right] \\ & \text { or } p=\frac{W}{V M} R T \\ & \text { or } p=\frac{d R T}{M} \quad\left[\text { Here, density }(d)=\frac{\text { Mass }}{\text { Volume }}\right] \\ & \therefore d=\frac{p M}{R T}=\frac{3 \times 28}{0.082 \times 503}=\frac{84}{41.246}=2.03 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mL} \end{aligned}$$

content: Identify the pair of gases that have equal rates of diffusion
explanation:
At constant pressure and temperature, the rate of diffusion or effusion of gas is inversely proportional to the square root of its density.

Rate of diffusion $$ \propto {1 \over {\sqrt d }}$$

$${{{r_1}} \over {{r_2}}} = \sqrt {{{{d_2}} \over {{d_1}}}} = \sqrt {{{{M_2}} \over {{M_1}}}} $$

$${r_1} = {r_2}$$ only when $${M_1} = {M_2}$$

Hence, N$$_2$$O and CO$$_2$$ have equal rate of diffusion due to equal molecular weight.

options.content: takes place at constant pressure and temperature

explanation: Phase transformation involve the energy changes e.g., ice requires heat for melting. Normally this melting takes place at constant pressure and during phase change, temperature remains constant (at 273 K).

content: The density of a gas A is thrice that of a gas B at the same temperature. The molecular weight of gas B is twice that of A. What will be the ratio of pressure acting on B and A?
explanation:
$$\frac{d}{p}=\frac{M}{RT}$$

Let density of gas B be d.

$$\therefore$$ Density of gas A = 3d

and let molecular weight of A be M.

$$\because$$ Molecular weight of B = 2M.

Since, R is a gas constant and T is same for both gases, so,

$${p_A} = {{{d_A}RT} \over {{M_A}}}$$ and $${p_B} = {{{d_B}RT} \over {{M_B}}}$$

$${{{p_B}} \over {{p_A}}} = {{{d_B}} \over {{d_A}}} \times {{{M_A}} \over {{M_B}}} = {d \over {3d}} \times {M \over {2M}} = {1 \over 6}$$

content: Gram molecular volume of oxygen at STP is
explanation:
Gram molecular volume of oxygen at STP is 22400 cm$$^{3}$$.

i.e. volume of 1 mole of oxygen molecule

$$=\frac{22400}{1}=22400$$ cm$$^{3}$$.

content: Dalton’s law of partial pressures is applicable to which one of the following systems?
explanation:
Dalton's law is applicable to mixture of gases, when they are non-reacting, so that the total pressure of all gases is equal to the sum of the partial pressure of each gas. CO and H$$_2$$ are non-reacting gases.

$$\therefore \quad p_T=p_{CO}+p_{H_2}$$

content: One mole of oxygen at 273 K and one mole of sulphur dioxide at 546 K are taken in two separate containers, then

options.content: kinetic energy of O$$_2$$ > kinetic energy of SO$$_2$$
kinetic energy of O$$_2$$ < kinetic energy of SO$$_2$$
kinetic energy of both are equal

explanation:
O$$_2$$ is diatomic and degree of freedom is 5.

$$\therefore$$ Kinetic energy of $$O_2=\frac{5}{2}\times k \times T$$

$$=\frac{5}{2}\times273\times k$$

SO$$_2$$ is triatomic and degree of freedom is 7.

$$\therefore$$ Kinetic energy of $$SO_2=\frac{7}{2}\times546\times k$$

$$\therefore$$ So, kinetic energy of SO$$_2$$ is more than that of O$$_2$$ i.e. SO$$_2$$ > O$$_2$$
